text: The ACTN3 RR genotype and R allele are overrepresented in power athletes compared to both endurance athletes and non-athletes, suggesting functional α-actinin-3 expression in fast-twitch fibers benefits strength and power performance.
The ACTN3 XX genotype frequency is higher in endurance athletes than in power athletes, suggesting the absence of α-actinin-3 may be neutral or beneficial for endurance performance.
specific_value: OR 0.63 (95% CI 0.52-0.76) for XX in power vs endurance athletes

text: The X allele of ACTN3 R577X is significantly more frequent in endurance athletes compared to power athletes, indicating a potential endurance advantage from reduced α-actinin-3 expression.
specific_value: OR 0.76 (95% CI 0.64-0.90, p=0.002) for X allele in power vs endurance

text: The RR genotype frequency is significantly higher in power athletes than in non-athletic controls, supporting a genetic component to power sport selection.
ACTN3 R577X genotype and allele frequencies are similar between male and female power athletes, indicating no sex-based difference in this genetic association with power performance.
Among power athletes, the heterozygous RX genotype is the most prevalent ACTN3 genotype (RX > RR > XX), suggesting heterozygosity may balance power and other athletic qualities.
The R allele is nearly three times more frequent than the X allele in power athletes, reinforcing that functional α-actinin-3 is associated with power-oriented athletic performance.
